free_text: Study of some reactions of 14.8 MeV deuterons with the
lithium isotopes
Energy spread 40 keV
Most of the work was done with the metallic targets
prepared by evaporation of isotopically enriched
lithium onto silver backings. Both thicknesses
were 1 mg/cm**2
The external deuteron beam was focused and
analyzed by two magnetic spectrometers.
The reaction products are selected
by a third magnetic spectrometer which can be rotated
about the target, and are detected either with a
scintillation counter or in nuclear emulsions.
Nuclear emulsions.
- the cross section at
the peak of the angular distribution.
- external deuteron
beam analysis
- detecting system
The absolute cross section scales were
obtained by comparison with monitor reaction and are
estimated to be uncertain to +-25%.
Compilation produced by Arzamas RFNC-VNIIEF
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
SD: BIB, ERR-ANALYS and REACTION code were
updated in all Subents.  DATA-ERR2 -> ERR-2.
ANG2-ERR -> ANG-ERR. MONITOR/REF were added.
SD: Corrections Subents 007, 009. Numerical
data revised. Subents 012, 013 added.
SD: Major revision in Subent 008.
The error bars represent estimated upper
limits on the errors of the relative cross sections.
(The relative cross sections of different reactions
are estimated to be accurate to ~10%.)
The excitation energy and total width of this level
were not measured accurately in this experiment but
are consistent with the values quoted by Browne (see
REL-REF)
